sections.content:
The code executed 3 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP, UP, UP.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 236, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Level 1. Pink 6x6 character at left edge of a dark room. Three 4x4 colored blocks on the right wall: red (top, row 19-22), blue (middle, row 25-28), green/N (bottom, row 31-34). A white tile path extends right from the character into the room. Bottom HUD bar shows order: pink, red, green, blue.
- Goal model: Visit/touch the colored blocks in the order shown on the bottom bar: red, then green, then blue.
- Action model: RIGHT/LEFT/UP/DOWN move the character. Need to probe movement.
- Plan: Move RIGHT to enter the room, then navigate to the red block first.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 235 actions; a strong score needs about 61 or fewer. You are 3.9x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Very interesting! Looking at the transitions:
- Transition 0 (UP): Changed row 53 - this is the HUD bar at the bottom
- Transition 1 (ACTION7): Changed rows 30-39 - character moved from 30-35 to 36-41
- Transition 2 (UP): Changed rows 30-39 - character moved from 36-41 to 30-35
- Transition 3 (ACTION7): Changed rows 30-39 - character moved from 30-35 to 36-41
- Transition 4 (UP): Changed rows 30-39 - character moved from 36-41 to 30-35
So ACTION7 toggles between 30-35 and 36-41. UP from 36-41 goes to 30-35. UP from 30-35 does nothing (only changes HUD).
The path is at rows 32-33, which doesn't overlap with any block. The blocks are at 13-16, 19-22, 25-28.
I'm stuck at 30-35 and can't reach 24-29 because green at 25-28 blocks the way.
Wait - let me reconsider the whole game. Maybe I've been misunderstanding the mechanic. Let me look at what the path does more carefully.
The path extends from col 16 to col 46. The blocks are at cols 42-45. So the path overlaps horizontally with the blocks. But the path is at rows 32-33, which doesn't overlap with any block vertically.
What if the game mechanic is:
1. The character moves UP/DOWN to select which "lane" the path is in
2. RIGHT/LEFT extends/shortens the path
3. When the path reaches a block (both horizontally and vertically), the block is "activated"
But I can't reach the block lanes because blocks block movement.
Wait, what if I'm wrong about blocks blocking movement? Let me test: what if the character CAN move to 24-29 despite green at 25-28? Earlier UP from 30-35 had no_impact, but maybe that was because of a different reason.
Actually, looking at the transition data more carefully:
- Transition 0 (UP from 30-35): Changed row 53 (HUD only)
- This means UP from 30-35 has no gameplay effect (only HUD changed)
So UP from 30-35 is indeed blocked. The character can't move to 24-29 because green is in the way.
Let me think about this differently. What if I need to use the path to interact with blocks from a distance? Like a laser beam that can affect blocks even when the character is not aligned?
But the path is at rows 32-33, and blocks are at 13-16, 19-22, 25-28. The path doesn't overlap with any block.
Wait, what if I need to MOVE the blocks to where the path is? What if ACTION7 from 30-35 (no overlap) does something different than ACTION7 from 24-29 (overlap)?
From earlier:
- ACTION7 from 24-29 (overlap with green): shifted blocks up AND moved character to 18-23
- ACTION7 from 30-35 (no overlap): toggled to 36-41
- ACTION7 from 36-41 (no overlap): toggled to 30-35
So ACTION7 behavior depends on whether the character overlaps with a block.
Hmm, what if I need to first extend the path to reach the blocks, then do something? But the path already extends to col 46, past the blocks at 42-45.
Wait, let me re-examine the path. The path is at rows 32-33, cols 16-46. The blocks are at rows 13-16, 19-22, 25-28, cols 42-45. The path overlaps horizontally but not vertically.
What if the path needs to be at the SAME row as a block to interact? And I need to move the character to align the path with a block?
But I can't reach 24-29 because green blocks UP. So I can't align the path with green.
UNLESS... what if I can move green out of the way first? How?
Wait, what if I need to use ACTION7 from a position that overlaps with a DIFFERENT block? Like, what if I can reach 18-23 (overlaps blue) or 12-17 (overlaps red)?
From 30-35, UP is blocked. From 36-41, UP goes to 30-35. So I can't reach above 30-35.
Hmm, but what if I can reach 18-23 or 12-17 by some other means? What if there's a teleport or warp mechanic?
Actually, let me reconsider. What if the character doesn't need to physically reach the blocks? What if the path can interact with blocks from a distance?
Let me try: from 30-35, press RIGHT to see if the path interacts with any block. Earlier RIGHT from 30-35 had no_impact=True. But maybe that was because the path was already at max length?
Wait, let me check: the path is at rows 32-33, which doesn't overlap with any block. So RIGHT has no effect because there's no block to interact with.
OK let me try a completely different approach. What if I need to:
1. Move the character to 36-41
2. Press ACTION7 to toggle to 30-35
3. Press UP (blocked, but maybe it does something else)
4. Press ACTION7 again
Actually, I already tried this and it just toggles between 30-35 and 36-41.
Let me think about the gray tiles on the left. They're at cols 0-9, rows 53-53 (gray bar) and rows 54-63 (dark area). These might be interactive.
Actually wait, let me look at the left side of the board more carefully. There are gray tiles at the left edge. What if they're buttons or switches?
Let me check the segmentation for objects on the left side.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me look at ALL objects on the board more carefully
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("All objects:")
for n in seg:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    if min(rows) < 55 or min(cols) < 10:  # Focus on left side and bottom
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, hash={n['hash'][:8]}")

# Let me also check the ASCII for the left side more carefully
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nLeft side (cols 0-15):")
for r in range(10, 50):
    row_str = ascii_lines[r][:16]
    if row_str.strip() and row_str != 'BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB':
        print(f"  Row {r}: {row_str}")

# Let me also check: what if I can use MOUSE on the gray tiles on the left?
# Or what if I need to use ACTION7 in a specific sequence?
# Let me try: from 30-35, extend path with RIGHT (no_impact), then try something else

# Actually, let me try MOUSE on the gray tiles at the left
# Gray tiles appear to be at rows ~12-42, col ~3-4
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 12, 'col': 3}])
print(f"\nAfter MOUSE(12, 3):")
print(f"  gameplay_changed: {last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}, no_impact: {last_action_result.get('no_impact')}")

# Check if anything changed
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
blocks2 = [(n['color'], min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in seg2 if n['color'] in 'RbN' and n['pixels'] >= 10 and n['boundary'][0][0] < 50]
print(f"  Blocks: {blocks2}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

print(f"\nAfter MOUSE(12, 3):")
print(f"  gameplay_changed: {last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}, no_impact: {last_action_result.get('no_impact')}")

# Check if anything changed
seg2 = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
blocks2 = [(n['color'], min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in seg2 if n['color'] in 'RbN' and n['pixels'] >= 10 and n['boundary'][0][0] < 50]
print(f"  Blocks: {blocks2}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
